cargo: cigarettes wearing three states' tax stamps
blackout_graffiti: I MET MY NEIGHBORS IN THE DARK
THE METER STOPPED. THE BILL DIDN'T.
flood_graffiti: THE RIVER TOOK THE LOBBY FIRST
WE BAILED. NOBODY BAILED US OUT.
THE HARBOR WANTS ITS LOT BACK
turf_graffiti: PAY THE CORNER, NOT THE CITY
THE BLOCK ANSWERS TO THE BLOCK
GUESTS CHECK IN AT THE CORNER
transit_graffiti: THE G WENT FIRST. REMEMBER THE G.
FOUR HOURS TO MIDTOWN, PACK LUNCH
exodus_graffiti: TAKE THE STOOP, IT'S GOOD LUCK
GONE UPSTATE TO BE SOMEONE'S PROBLEM
THE MOVING TRUCK ATE MY DEPOSIT
WE LEFT THE KEYS IN THE RIVER
vision_graffiti: THE DOOR IS ON THE LAST CAR
I WOKE UP KNOWING THE TIDE
THE LIGHT UNDER FULTON IS REAL
radio_content: a man listing bridges in the order they'll go
shipping forecasts for the old coastline
a child's voice doing the stock report, accurately
two hours of tide tables, sung
apologies, very specific ones, from nobody who gives a name
the old station IDs, played at the wrong speed, on purpose
miracle_desc: a hydrant that ran clean through the bad water week, only for children
frost on one window spelling a name the tenant wouldn't say
the ferry horn answering a funeral, three times, no ferry in sight
a blackout candle that burned nine days on one inch of wax
pigeons spelling a letter, briefly, photographed badly
the same dog at three corners at once, sworn to by separate witnesses
surge_flotsam: a confession booth, door latched from inside
a deli counter, scale still accurate
an upright freezer full of wedding cake
the pawnshop safe, open and empty and polished
a carousel horse, still smiling, missing a leg
relic_examples: rebar from the seawall breach, tied with ribbon
the last farecard sold, laminated, disputed
bolts off the old boardwalk wheel
a slice of the truce table, lacquered
transformer glass from the night the grid died
harbor pearls, which are oyster buttons, which is not advertised
tabloid_heat: BLACKTOP BUBBLES, BROKER SELLS THE VIEW
